data.title: Modine’s Pure Play Climate Shift Meets Surging AI Data Center Demand
data.description:
• Modine Manufacturing (NYSE:MOD) has completed its shift into a pure-play climate solutions manufacturer following the spin-off of its Performance Technologies segment.
  • The company is reporting strong momentum in AI data center cooling solutions, with long-term agreements anticipated with major hyperscalers.
  • This move reshapes Modine’s business profile around higher margin climate and infrastructure opportunities tied to AI growth.

For investors watching NYSE:MOD, the company now presents a more focused story around climate solutions and critical infrastructure. The share price sits at $216.5, and the stock has delivered very large 3 year and 5 year returns, with 1 year returns of 133.6% and year to date performance of 53.8%. Over the past month, the stock is up 58.2%, while the 7 day move has been 0.4%.

The AI data center push and anticipated long-term agreements with hyperscalers could give Modine more visibility on demand for its climate systems and may reduce some order volatility. As the company leans into this new profile, investors can watch how the pure-play climate focus, contract structure and margin mix evolve in future updates.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Modine Manufacturing by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Modine Manufacturing.

📰 Beyond the headline: 3 risks and 2 things going right for Modine Manufacturing that every investor should see.

For Modine, becoming a pure-play climate solutions business lines the company up squarely with long-term themes like data center buildout and energy efficiency, but it also concentrates the bet. The recent third quarter update shows sales of US$805 million compared to US$616.8 million a year ago, yet the company reported a net loss of US$47.4 million versus net income of US$41 million a year earlier. Management still raised fiscal 2026 net sales guidance to a 20% to 25% increase, which indicates they see solid demand across the focused portfolio, including AI data center cooling. The completed share buyback of 230,000 shares for US$18.4 million is relatively small in the context of the overall equity story, so capital allocation now looks more centered on capacity, products and execution in data centers and HVAC. As Modine signs or negotiates long-term agreements with hyperscalers, contract terms, pricing, and any required upfront investment will be key for you to watch because they will influence how quickly this business mix translates into more resilient earnings rather than just higher revenue.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Modine Manufacturing Narrative

  • The shift to pure-play climate solutions and strong AI data center demand lines up directly with the narrative that data center and energy-efficient thermal solutions can be a major growth driver for Modine.
  • The current net loss for the quarter and lower net income over nine months compared to a year ago test the narrative that expansion into high-tech verticals cleanly improves margins in the near term.
  • The raised 2026 net sales outlook and discussed long-term hyperscaler agreements add detail on future demand visibility that is not fully captured in the broader narrative about customer pipelines and backlog.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Modine Manufacturing to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Profit margins are currently under pressure, with profit margins of 3.4% being lower than 6.3% a year ago, and recent results affected by large one off items.
  • ⚠️ Debt is not well covered by operating cash flow, which could limit flexibility if the company continues to invest heavily in capacity and AI data center projects.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow 69.28% per year according to analysts, which, if achieved, would support the case for a more focused, higher quality business mix.
  • 🎁 The shares are trading at 15.4% below the Simply Wall St fair value estimate, which some investors may see as a margin between price and intrinsic value.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, keep an eye on how quickly Modine turns AI data center momentum and its higher 2026 sales outlook into cleaner profitability and cash flow. The structure of any long-term hyperscaler contracts, including volumes, pricing and service obligations, will tell you a lot about earnings visibility. You might also want to track whether profit margins start to move back toward prior levels as one off costs roll off and new facilities, such as the Franklin, Wisconsin plant, ramp up. Finally, given analysts have flagged three key risks and two rewards, watching debt metrics, free cash flow and the pace of further divestitures will help you assess whether the pure-play climate focus is translating into a more resilient business over time.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Royal Gold (RGLD) After Its Strong Multi‑Year Rally?
data.description:
• If you are asking yourself whether Royal Gold is still fairly priced after its strong run, this article will walk through what the current share price might be implying.
  • Royal Gold's share price last closed at US$286.10, with stated returns of 2.5% over 7 days, 7.9% over 30 days, 29.4% year to date, 96.7% over 1 year, 143.0% over 3 years and 175.3% over 5 years.
  • Recent coverage around Royal Gold has focused on its role as a precious metals royalty and streaming company, which can behave differently to traditional miners when sentiment around commodities shifts. This context helps frame why the stock's performance profile looks the way it does and why investors are paying attention to how its business model is being valued.
  • Despite that backdrop, Royal Gold currently has a valuation score of 1 out of 6. We will look at what different valuation methods say about the stock and then finish with a way of thinking about valuation that goes beyond the usual models.

Royal Gold scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Royal Gold Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today using a required rate of return, to arrive at an estimate of what the business might be worth per share.

For Royal Gold, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow figure is given as $13.09 million. Analysts have provided explicit free cash flow projections through to 2029, with Simply Wall St extrapolating additional estimates out to 2035. For example, projected free cash flow for 2029 is $1,289.00 million, and the ten year projection set ranges between about $1,284.79 million and $1,514.00 million before discounting those values back to today.

Putting all of those discounted cash flows together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $272.73 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $286.10, this suggests the stock is roughly 4.9% above this estimate on this measure, which is a relatively small gap.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

Royal Gold is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Royal Gold.

Approach 2: Royal Gold Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Royal Gold, the P/E ratio is a useful way to link what you pay for each share to the earnings that support that price. It gives you a quick shorthand for how many years of current earnings the market is willing to pay for.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on what investors expect for future growth and how much risk they see in those earnings. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually means investors look for a lower multiple.

Royal Gold currently trades on a P/E of 50.30x, compared with a Metals and Mining industry average of about 25.28x and a peer average of 26.57x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Royal Gold is 53.34x, which is its proprietary estimate of what the P/E might be given factors like earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk profile.

Because the Fair Ratio directly incorporates those company specific drivers, it can be more tailored than a simple comparison to peers or the broad industry. With Royal Gold’s actual P/E of 50.30x sitting below the Fair Ratio of 53.34x, this approach suggests the shares may be undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. On Simply Wall St you can use Narratives, which let you set out your own story for Royal Gold by linking assumptions for future revenue, earnings and margins to a financial forecast. You can then compare your Fair Value to today’s price and watch that view update automatically as new news or earnings arrive, all while seeing how different investors in the Community can look at the same company and reach very different Fair Values. data.title: Interpump Group (BIT:IP) Margin Slippage Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narratives
data.description:
Interpump Group (BIT:IP) closed FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of €494.6 million and basic EPS of €0.35, while trailing twelve month figures showed revenue of about €2.1 billion and EPS of €1.96, setting a clear earnings season marker for investors. The company has seen quarterly revenue hold in a tight band between €494.6 million and €555.3 million through 2025, with basic EPS ranging from €0.35 to €0.56 over the same stretch. The latest results slot into an already data rich year. With a trailing net margin of 10.1% compared with 10.9% a year earlier, the focus now is on whether the current profitability profile supports the growth expectations embedded in the stock.

See our full analysis for Interpump Group.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to align them with the key narratives around Interpump Group to see where the recent results support the prevailing stories and where they start to challenge them.

See what the community is saying about Interpump Group

Advertisement

EPS Trend Softens While TTM Stays Near €2

  • Basic EPS for FY 2025 moved between €0.35 and €0.56 per quarter, with the latest trailing twelve month EPS at €1.96 compared with €2.12 a year earlier, so the recent print sits slightly below the prior trailing level.
  • Analysts' consensus view talks about earnings growth potential, yet the trailing numbers show a flatter picture, with TTM EPS at €1.96 alongside a 10.1% net margin, which sits just below last year’s 10.9%, so the growth story is being set against earnings that have not yet moved meaningfully ahead of the previous year.

Revenue Holds Around €2.1b While Hydraulics Risks Linger

  • Over the last six quarters, total revenue has stayed in a relatively tight range between €489.9 million and €555.3 million per quarter, with trailing twelve month revenue of about €2.1b compared with €2.08b to €2.11b in the earlier trailing data points provided.
  • Critics highlight a cautious narrative around Hydraulics, noting seven consecutive quarters of organic revenue decline and low visibility on true end demand, and the largely steady revenue band, combined with a modest margin slip from 10.9% to 10.1%, leaves that bearish concern intact rather than clearly contradicted.
    • The consensus narrative points to expanding opportunities in water management and infrastructure, yet the revenue figures presented cluster tightly around €2.1b instead of showing any clear acceleration.
    • Comments around exceptional Water Jetting growth in China being a one off also sit awkwardly with the stable revenue run rate, which does not show any outsized step up despite that contract.
If you are weighing that tension between Hydraulics weakness and group level stability, skeptics' concerns and bulls' hopes are both on the table in the dedicated bear case for Interpump, which unpacks how these operating trends could affect future cash generation and valuation. 🐻 Interpump Group Bear Case

Mixed Signals Between P/E, DCF Fair Value And Price Target

  • The shares trade on a trailing P/E of 21.2x versus peers at 28.4x and the European Machinery industry at 21.6x, while a DCF fair value of €33.69 sits below the current share price of €41.50 and below an analyst price target of €51.95.
  • What stands out for the bullish side is that forecasts call for earnings to grow around 12% per year against revenue growth of about 5.2% per year, so bulls argue that faster earnings growth could justify both the current P/E and the €51.95 target even though the DCF fair value points to a lower figure.
    • The current margin of 10.1%, only slightly under the prior 10.9%, is one of the pillars used in the optimistic narrative that profitability can improve further, yet the margin slippage in the historical data means that view relies heavily on future improvements rather than what is already in the numbers.
    • At the same time, the stock’s higher three month volatility than the Italian market gives bears a concrete data point when they argue that paying above the €33.69 DCF fair value could expose investors to sharper price swings if earnings do not track those optimistic forecasts.
If you want to see how optimistic investors build a case around those growth forecasts, margins and valuation gaps, the full bull narrative on Interpump walks through the numbers behind that thesis. 🐂 Interpump Group Bull Case

data.title: Nippon Air conditioning Services' (TSE:4658) Upcoming Dividend Will Be Larger Than Last Year's
data.description:
Nippon Air conditioning Services Co., Ltd.'s (TSE:4658) dividend will be increasing from last year's payment of the same period to ¥29.00 on 26th of June. This takes the annual payment to 1.8% of the current stock price, which is about average for the industry.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Nippon Air conditioning Services' Payment Could Potentially Have Solid Earnings Coverage

Unless the payments are sustainable, the dividend yield doesn't mean too much. Prior to this announcement, Nippon Air conditioning Services' dividend was comfortably covered by both cash flow and earnings. This means that a large portion of its earnings are being retained to grow the business.

Over the next year, EPS could expand by 14.6% if recent trends continue. Assuming the dividend continues along recent trends, we think the payout ratio could be 51% by next year, which is in a pretty sustainable range.

Check out our latest analysis for Nippon Air conditioning Services

Dividend Volatility

While the company has been paying a dividend for a long time, it has cut the dividend at least once in the last 10 years. The annual payment during the last 10 years was ¥15.00 in 2016, and the most recent fiscal year payment was ¥26.00. This works out to be a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 5.7% a year over that time. It's good to see the dividend growing at a decent rate, but the dividend has been cut at least once in the past. Nippon Air conditioning Services might have put its house in order since then, but we remain cautious.

The Dividend Looks Likely To Grow

With a relatively unstable dividend, it's even more important to evaluate if earnings per share is growing, which could point to a growing dividend in the future. Nippon Air conditioning Services has impressed us by growing EPS at 15% per year over the past five years. Shareholders are getting plenty of the earnings returned to them, which combined with strong growth makes this quite appealing.

Nippon Air conditioning Services Looks Like A Great Dividend Stock

In summary, it is always positive to see the dividend being increased, and we are particularly pleased with its overall sustainability. Earnings are easily covering distributions, and the company is generating plenty of cash. Taking this all into consideration, this looks like it could be a good dividend opportunity.

It's important to note that companies having a consistent dividend policy will generate greater investor confidence than those having an erratic one. data.title: Bharat Forge Limited Just Beat EPS By 22%: Here's What Analysts Think Will Happen Next
data.description:
As you might know, Bharat Forge Limited (NSE:BHARATFORG) just kicked off its latest second-quarter results with some very strong numbers. It was overall a positive result, with revenues beating expectations by 7.6% to hit ₹40b. Bharat Forge also reported a statutory profit of ₹6.26, which was an impressive 22% above what the analysts had forecast. This is an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance in its report, look at what experts are forecasting for next year, and see if there has been any change to expectations for the business. With this in mind, we've gathered the latest statutory forecasts to see what the analysts are expecting for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

After the latest results, the 23 analysts covering Bharat Forge are now predicting revenues of ₹164.8b in 2026. If met, this would reflect a modest 2.2% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to ascend 14% to ₹27.03. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of ₹162.7b and earnings per share (EPS) of ₹27.00 in 2026. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

Check out our latest analysis for Bharat Forge

The consensus price target rose 22% to ₹1,644despite there being no meaningful change to earnings estimates. It could be that the analystsare reflecting the predictability of Bharat Forge's earnings by assigning a price premium. There's another way to think about price targets though, and that's to look at the range of price targets put forward by analysts, because a wide range of estimates could suggest a diverse view on possible outcomes for the business. There are some variant perceptions on Bharat Forge, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at ₹2,149 and the most bearish at ₹930 per share. This is a fairly broad spread of estimates, suggesting that analysts are forecasting a wide range of possible outcomes for the business.

One way to get more context on these forecasts is to look at how they compare to both past performance, and how other companies in the same industry are performing. It's pretty clear that there is an expectation that Bharat Forge's revenue growth will slow down substantially, with revenues to the end of 2026 expected to display 4.4% growth on an annualised basis. This is compared to a historical growth rate of 16% over the past five years. By way of comparison, the other companies in this industry with analyst coverage are forecast to grow their revenue at 11% per year. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it seems obvious that Bharat Forge is also expected to grow slower than other industry participants.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most obvious conclusion is that there's been no major change in the business' prospects in recent times, with the analysts holding their earnings forecasts steady, in line with previous estimates. On the plus side, there were no major changes to revenue estimates; although forecasts imply they will perform worse than the wider industry. We note an upgrade to the price target, suggesting that the analysts believes the intrinsic value of the business is likely to improve over time.

With that said, the long-term trajectory of the company's earnings is a lot more important than next year. We have estimates - from multiple Bharat Forge analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

• Solid balance sheet
• Earnings recovered, with positive outlook.
• SYK is fair priced.
• ROE over industry average (past and future)

Stryker has a solid balance sheet. I always want to see more equity than debt, which is the case with $19.2b (equity) versus $12.9b (debt) and leads to a debt to equity ratio of 67%. If we even take the cash position into account, which is $2.4b, the debt may be reduced to $10.5b, and we end up with a net debt to equity ratio of 54%.

The earnings finally recovered after the drop caused by Corona pandemic, so we are in 2024 back at $3.4b of earnings. According to the analysts the positive trend should continue, with increasing EPS at an EPS Growth rate of 10.6% and an EPS E2026 of $13. And all that we can still get at its fair price? The fair price is calculated on a DCF model, based on the future Free Cash Flows. I use to focus on today’s FCF compared to in 3Y:
FCF 2024: $3.5b
FCF 2027: $5.0b
And there we have it, SYK must deliver very well within the next three years.

I always have a look on return on equity ROE:
With a ROE of 17.5% and future ROE of 21.9%, my money is working well.
Future Earnings growth rate is 10.5%, ok for an easy ride.

Fair Value: With 6% return p.a. and discount rate of 6%, I get a fair value of $323.
